lessons.title: Auto Memory: Taming the 200-Line MEMORY.md Limit
lessons.summary: How to structure Claude Code's auto memory system using topic files and the capture-promote-prune cycle to stay under the 200-line MEMORY.md limit.

lessons.title: The Three-Level CLAUDE.md Hierarchy
lessons.summary: How CLAUDE.md operates at Global, Project, and Directory levels — and why the most specific level always wins.

lessons.title: Hooks: Auto-Run Scripts Before and After Every Tool Call
lessons.summary: How to configure preTool and postTool hooks in settings.json to auto-format code, enforce policies, and audit tool usage.

lessons.title: Tame Context Bloat with /compact Focus Hints
lessons.summary: How to use /compact with focus hints at natural breakpoints to preserve critical context before auto-compression decides for you.

lessons.title: Worktrees: Run Parallel Claude Sessions Without Conflicts
lessons.summary: How to use the --worktree flag to run multiple Claude Code sessions on the same repo in isolated checkouts, enabling parallel work without file conflicts.

lessons.title: Custom Slash Commands: Build Reusable Prompt Templates
lessons.summary: How to create custom slash commands as markdown files in .claude/commands/, use $ARGUMENTS for flexible templates, and share them with your team via git.

lessons.title: Code Review: Multi-Agent PR Analysis That Catches What Humans Miss
lessons.summary: How Claude Code Review dispatches parallel specialized agents to find logic errors, security issues, and regressions in your PRs with under 1% false positive rate.

lessons.title: Auto Mode: Smarter Permission Handling Without --dangerously-skip-permissions
lessons.summary: How Claude Code's auto mode uses a two-stage classifier to auto-approve safe actions and block dangerous ones, eliminating approval fatigue without sacrificing safety.

lessons.title: Put Claude on Autopilot with /loop
lessons.summary: How to use the /loop command to schedule recurring tasks like deploy monitoring, PR babysitting, and test runs — so Claude watches while you focus on real work.

lessons.title: Plan Mode: Think First, Code Second
lessons.summary: How to use Plan Mode to separate thinking from execution — iterate on the approach in read-only mode, then let Claude one-shot the implementation.

lessons.title: Remote Control: Drive Claude Code From Your Phone
lessons.summary: How to use /remote-control and /teleport to drive a local Claude Code session from your phone or any browser — your code stays on your machine, only messages travel through the bridge.

lessons.title: Give Claude a Feedback Loop to 2-3x Output Quality
lessons.summary: The #1 tip from Claude Code's creator: let Claude verify its own work with tests, a browser, or simulators so it iterates to correct results instead of guessing once.

lessons.title: NO_FLICKER Mode: Kill Terminal Flashing With One Env Var
lessons.summary: Enable CLAUDE_CODE_NO_FLICKER=1 to switch to a fullscreen alternate-screen renderer that eliminates flickering, keeps memory flat, and adds mouse support.

lessons.title: Output Styles: Change How Claude Code Talks to You
lessons.summary: Use /config to switch between Default, Explanatory, Learning, and Concise output styles — or create your own custom style as a Markdown file.

lessons.title: /batch: Parallel Agents for Large-Scale Code Migrations
lessons.summary: Use /batch to decompose big refactors into parallel agents running in isolated worktrees — each one tested, reviewed, and PR'd automatically.

lessons.title: /simplify: Auto-Refactor Your Changes Before You Ship
lessons.summary: Run /simplify to have Claude review your changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency — then fix issues automatically before you commit.

lessons.title: Plugins: Extend Claude Code from the Built-in Marketplace
lessons.summary: Use /plugin to browse, install, and manage plugin packages that bundle skills, hooks, and config — with User, Project, and Local scopes.

lessons.title: /btw: Ask Questions Mid-Task Without Breaking Flow
lessons.summary: Use /btw to ask side questions while Claude works — full context visibility, zero interruption, minimal cost.

lessons.title: Custom Agents: Build Specialized AI Teammates in .claude/agents
lessons.summary: Create purpose-built agents with custom tools, permissions, and models as Markdown files — then share them with your team via git.

lessons.title: /ultraplan: Plan in the Cloud, Execute Anywhere
lessons.summary: Use /ultraplan to offload complex planning to a cloud web UI with inline comments and structured review, then execute back in your terminal or in the cloud.

lessons.title: /voice: Code by Talking Instead of Typing
lessons.summary: Use /voice to speak your prompts — mix voice and text, choose push-to-talk or hands-free, and transcription is completely free.

lessons.title: Routines: Claude Code Agents That Run While You Sleep
lessons.summary: Set up routines to run Claude Code automatically on a schedule, via API, or in response to GitHub events — all on Anthropic's cloud.

lessons.title: MCP Servers: Connect Claude Code to Slack, Databases, and Any API
lessons.summary: Configure .mcp.json to give Claude Code access to Slack, Postgres, Sentry, and more — then share the setup with your whole team via git.

lessons.title: Chrome Extension: Give Claude Eyes for Frontend Work
lessons.summary: Use the Chrome extension and /chrome to let Claude Code see, click, and debug your UI — turning blind code generation into a visual feedback loop.

lessons.title: /effort: Five Levels of Reasoning Depth on Demand
lessons.summary: Use /effort to switch between low, medium, high, xhigh, and max reasoning depth — match token spend to task complexity in real time.

lessons.title: Custom Status Lines: A Live Dashboard Below Your Prompt
lessons.summary: Use /statusline to display model, cost, context usage, and git branch in a persistent bar below your Claude Code prompt.

lessons.title: Agent Teams: Orchestrate a Swarm of Claude Sessions on One Task
lessons.summary: Enable Agent Teams to coordinate multiple Claude Code sessions with shared task lists, dependency tracking, and inter-agent messaging for cross-layer features.

lessons.title: Security Review: AI-Powered SAST on Every Pull Request
lessons.summary: Add the Claude Code Security Review GitHub Action to catch logic-level vulnerabilities that pattern-based scanners miss — setup takes about 10 minutes.
